name: White Chocolate Devils
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Place a baking rack in the center of the oven and line a 12-muffin pan with paper cups.
2. In a medium bowl, sift together flour, cocoa powder, baking powder, and salt. Mix until blended; set aside.
3. In an electric mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat butter and sugar at medium speed until thoroughly blended, about 2 minutes. Add egg and vanilla; mix for 1 minute until well blended.
4. Beat in melted chocolate and buttermilk one-third at a time, alternating with the dry ingredients. Mix just until combined.
5. Divide batter among prepared muffin cups and bake cupcakes for 25-30 minutes or until they spring back when lightly touched. Cool cupcakes in pan for 5 minutes, then remove to a wire rack and cool completely.
White Chocolate Filling:
1. Place white chocolate in a stainless steel bowl. In a saucepan, bring heavy cream to a gentle boil. Remove from heat and pour hot cream over the white chocolate. Let stand for 30 minutes to melt; whisk until smooth.
2. Cover bowl and refrigerate filling for at least 4 hours or until well chilled. Whip the chilled filling with an electric mixer until it reaches the consistency of sour cream.
Dark Chocolate Glaze:
1. Finely grind chocolate in a food processor fitted with metal chopping blades for 20-30 seconds.
2. In a small saucepan, bring cream and corn syrup to a gentle boil. With motor running, pour hot cream through feed tube onto chocolate. Process until smooth about 10 seconds.
3. Pour glaze into a medium bowl; cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes or until slightly thickened.
1. Remove cupcakes from refrigerator. Dip tops of each cupcake in glaze and allow excess to drip off. Place cupcakes back in the refrigerator for 10 minutes to set the glaze.
2. Remove cupcakes again and re-dip tops in glaze. Set glazed cupcakes aside at room temperature.
3. Melt white chocolate in microwave, stirring until shiny (1-4 minutes). Stir about 1½ minutes. Fill a small pastry bag or parchment cone with plain tip with melted white chocolate.
4. Drizzle chocolate in zigzag pattern over top of glazed cupcakes.
5. Chill before serving.
These are very good, rich but wonderful when you want to impress the crowd.
